title: pyrus × jackii
intro: Pyrus × jackii, also known as Jack's pear or Chinese pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family, resulting from a cross between Pyrus calleryana and Pyrus pyrifolia. The bark and leaves of this plant are used in traditional Chinese medicine, known for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to promote digestive health and reduce fever. The health benefits of Pyrus × jackii include its ability to aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Pyrus × jackii is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and may also be used in the form of a tincture or powder, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
